geometry_representation.base: Star-shaped radius field r(theta, phi). One radius per direction from center. Surface defined by this field evaluated over spherical directions.
geometry_representation.primary_primitive: Localized bump. Properties: local controllability, smooth blending, compact parameterization, stable evaluation.
geometry_representation.deprioritized: Global harmonic-only representations. Local appendage control mattered more than global shape modes.
invariants: Geometry must remain star-shaped relative to primary center.
Deformation is part of physical representation, not merely visual.

bending_model.key_insight: Appendage bends because evaluation frames rotate — NOT because global blob topology changes.

leaning.description: Whole bump direction tilts as mostly rigid directional offset.
leaning.use: Simple directional deformation, push/pull, low-cost articulation.

gradual_bending.description: Rotation varies progressively along bump extent. Farther regions experience greater frame rotation. Curvature emerges through transported rotated frames.

bending_model.rejection_reason: Breaks elegance, complicates collision, weakens unified field representation, risks becoming capsules glued together.

collision_model.operates_on: Transformed/deformed evaluation points after local frame transport.
collision_model.consequence: Collision geometry is evaluated after bending. Bending creates new effective collision points.
collision_model.sensory_constraint: Collision handling must preserve meaningful deformation patterns. Overly simplified responses (rigid clipping, proxy hulls, hard separation) destroy deformation gradients that encode sensory information.

physics_hierarchy.note: Creature orientation mandatory. Appendage directions must rotate with body motion.

rendering.target_aesthetic: Smooth volumetric soft bodies. Not polygon meshes.
rendering.artifact_problem: Camera-facing billboards cause scale/fiber/lizard-skin artifacts near sharp bumps, grazing angles, high-curvature regions.
rendering.sampling_artifact: Fibonacci lattice stretched non-uniformly by r(theta,phi). Particles near bump peak pushed farther out, creating visible rows at bump-sphere transition. Root cause: sampling distribution, not normals.
rendering.sampling_fix_correct: Importance sampling weighted by local surface area element |dp/dtheta x dp/dphi|.
rendering.sampling_fix_pragmatic: Oversample directions where r(theta,phi) > r_min + threshold. Compensates for stretching without full importance sampling.
rendering.sampling_fix_status: Deferred. Physics work prioritized over rendering quality at this stage.

sensory_deformation.principle: Deformation is part of information-processing architecture, not only collision response.
sensory_deformation.sensory_roles: Some blobs/protrusions serve primarily sensory roles: whiskers, antennae, pressure lobes, compliant feelers.
sensory_deformation.architectural_consequence: Geometry, sensing, collision, and deformation all emerge from the same analytical blob framework.

volume_pressure.model: Approximate volume preservation. Flattening in one region redistributes outward elsewhere.
volume_pressure.note: Not strict incompressibility. Pressure-like compensation behavior. Stiffness as unified parameter.

mathematical: Exact bend-limit derivation — depends on bump width, length, curvature rate, local radius gradient, neighboring transported frames. No fixed global limit.
Maintaining star-shaped validity under transported frames.
Efficient normal generation for curved bumps.
Transition between leaning and gradual bending.

session_stop_point.topic: Rendering fiber artifact at bump-sphere transition.
session_stop_point.last_reasoning: Artifact is a sampling distribution problem (Fibonacci lattice stretched by r(theta,phi)), not a normal problem. Correct fix: importance sampling by surface area element. Pragmatic fix: oversample bump region. Decision: deferred — physics work more valuable at this stage.

notes: Initial KB write from single-session work 2026-05-15. Session ran to token limit without writing. Reconstructed from ChatGPT summary.
